text:
Market today: The mood is cautious, driven by escalating geopolitical risks and potential trade tensions. This directly impacts your tech and chip heavy portfolio, as these sectors are sensitive to global stability and supply chain concerns.

Your holdings: SPAL is a major concern, being a new leveraged position down over 51%. SOXL, another leveraged chip ETF, is also down significantly (-46.24%). Remember, margin amplifies these drops. QQQ and SMH are also showing "SELL" signals, suggesting broad market and chip weakness.

Watch: Keep a close eye on the China AI export control news; it could further pressure your chip holdings. Also, monitor your stops on SOXL and SPAL, as leveraged ETFs can move fast.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management and consider trimming vulnerable, leveraged positions in this uncertain environment.
plays: Trim SOXL: Currently down -46.24%, this leveraged chip ETF is highly vulnerable. Consider reducing exposure to mitigate further losses.
Re-evaluate SPAL: This new leveraged ETF is down -51.33%. Given the deep loss and volatility, consider reducing its size or tightening its stop at 21.38.
Respect stops: For QQQ (stop 670) and SMH (stop 540), honor your predefined stops to manage risk in this uncertain market.
